title: Concurrent Cache Access, Duplicate Suppression, Ownership, and Race Evidence · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers concurrent cache access, duplicate suppression, ownership, and race evidence into a changed race-free duplicate-suppressed resource loader. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of race-free duplicate-suppressed resource loader with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progres…
objectives: Protect shared cache invariants with a mutex or owned goroutine and keep lock scope away from slow network work.
Represent one canonical in-flight fetch, waiters, completion, result, and cleanup without leaking entries.
Let each waiter cancel independently while defining when shared underlying work should continue or stop.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Cache Synchronizati…
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 7173 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove duplicate suppression, independent waiters, safe publication, and in-flight cleanup; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
